part3_passage.title: OVERSTAYING VIETNAM VISAS
part3_passage.text:
According to Vietnam Immigration policy, foreigners wishing to enter Vietnam legally are required to have a valid Vietnam visa. However, in some cases, because of unexpected reasons, visa holders do not exit Vietnam or extend the visa even their Vietnam visas expire. In this case, foreigners will be penalized for their actions.

If traveler’s visa has been expired no more than 3 days, besides visa extension fee, the holder must pay penalty fee for overstaying about 25 USD per day overstaying. For foreigners planning to exit Vietnam after overstaying for no more than 3 days, they can pay fine fee by themselves to Vietnam Immigration officer at ports of exit, and then leave the country.

If foreigner’s visa has been expired for a long time, they are to get an exit visa or visa extension immediately. Exit visa allows them a specified period to leave Vietnam legally. In order to get an exit visa or visa extension, visa holders need to submit their original passports, then pay the fine fee and fee for extension or exit one. Normally, it takes 7 to 10 days to get an exit or extended visa.

To avoid any problems while living or exiting Vietnam, foreigners are advised to pay attention to their Vietnam visa valid duration. If they want to stay in the country longer their visa temporary period, foreigners should contact Vietnam Immigration Department to arrange visa extension or visa renewal before expiration date.

part3_passage.title: TRAVEL FROM VIETNAM TO CAMBODIA
part3_passage.text:
Vietnam and Cambodia are neighbors in Southeast Asia on the Indochina Peninsula. The two countries share a long border with a variety of crossings. Travel between the two countries is remarkably easy, with a variety of ways to travel, including flights, buses, cars and boats.

Travel Documents

Visas are required to travel from Vietnam into Cambodia; however, they are available on arrival at airports and border crossings. In order to qualify for a visa on arrival, you must have two passport-sized photographs with you, although if you don't, you may simply be fined a small amount and granted a visa anyway.

Flights

Cambodia has two major airports, one in the capital, Phnom Penh (airport code PNH), and one in Siem Reap (REP), which is near the Angkor Wat temple complex. Most flights from Vietnam to Cambodia originate in Ho Chi Minh City and take between one to two hours. Arriving by air in Cambodia is relatively simple and the customs process is mostly hassle-free.

Land Crossings

Numerous land crossings are available between Vietnam and Cambodia. Many people travel from Ho Chi Minh City to Phnom Penh by bus, a trip that takes approximately six hours, including a border stop. This crossing is called Moc Bai on the Vietnamese side and Bavet on the Cambodian side. Additionally, at least seven other land border crossings are open between the two nations; private vehicles are allowed to cross at all points. Visas on arrival are available at most land border crossings.

Water Crossings

The Mekong River runs north to south through Cambodia before ending in Vietnam. River boat border crossings are available with brief stops on land to check passports and visas. Visas on arrival are available at the Cambodian border crossing. Fast boat service is available from Chau Doc in Vietnam to Phnom Penh. More leisurely cruises are also available, and service is available all the way to Siem Reap in the north central part of Cambodia.

part3_passage.title: CUSTOMS PROCEDURES TO ENTER AND EXIT VIETNAM
part3_passage.text:
Upon entering or exiting Vietnam, all visitors to Vietnam must complete the entry, exit and customs procedures. They have to fill out the new entry & exit form (CHY2000), submit to the Immigration Officers, show all the required documents (passport, plus visa approval letter & photos in case you use Vietnam visa on arrival) and wait a couple of minutes until the procedures are completed to enter or exit the country.

To quickly fulfill the entry/ exit form without any mistakes, please be noted with the following information: the goods and luggage brought to Vietnam must be sufficient for personal use only; luggage of travelers as declared at Customs on arrival must be shown again at Customs when leaving Vietnam (except for those having been consumed or given as gifts).

Remember to specify in the entry/exit form those following stuffs (if any): Video cameras, and electronic devices not for personal use; Gold, silver, and jewelry not for personal belongings; Foreign currency in cash (paper, coins and traveler’s cheques) over US$ 7,000; over 5,000,000 Vietnamese Dong in cash; Gold (over 300 g): If more than 3,000 g, you are required to deposit and re-export the surplus.

Prohibited goods for import or export from Vietnam (if without permit): weapons, ammunition, explosives, inflammables, firecrackers of all sorts, opium and other narcotics, toxic chemicals, antiques, rare fauna and flora, documents related to national security etc.

part3_passage.title: GUIDING TOURS FROM MOC BAI INTERNATIONAL
part3_passage.text:
BORDER GATE

Moc Bai border gate in Tay Ninh province is the largest international land border in the south, along the mainland of Vietnam - Cambodia.

Compared to the other border gates, Moc Bai has a special advantage because it is located on the trans-Asia road and only 70 km from Ho Chi Minh City. Along with tax-free supermarkets, Moc Bai has welcomed many tourists from Ho Chi Minh City and neighboring provinces to visit and shop.

Near the border gate, Thuan Loi tunnel - the revolutionary historical site - is also an attractive destination. In addition, visitors can come to Ba Den Mountain, the highest mountain in Southeastern region, symbolizing the land and people of Tay Ninh to enjoy a panoramic view of Tay Ninh town center.

About 7 km from Ha Tien town, Xa Xia or Ha Tien international border gate has long become a famous tourist attraction in Kien Giang.

part3_passage.title: TRAVEL FROM VIETNAM TO CAMBODIA
part3_passage.text:
Vietnam and Cambodia are neighbors in Southeast Asia on the Indochina Peninsula. The two countries share a long border with a variety of crossings. Travel between the two countries is remarkably easy, with a variety of ways to travel, including flights, buses, cars and boats.

Travel Documents

Visas are required to travel from Vietnam into Cambodia; however, they are available on arrival at airports and border crossings. In order to qualify for a visa on arrival, you must have two passport-sized photographs with you, although if you don't, you may simply be fined a small amount and granted a visa anyway.

Flights

Cambodia has two major airports, one in the capital, Phnom Penh (airport code PNH), and one in Siem Reap (REP), which is near the Angkor Wat temple complex. Most flights from Vietnam to Cambodia originate in Ho Chi Minh City and take between one to two hours.

Land Crossings

Numerous land crossings are available between Vietnam and Cambodia. Many people travel from Ho Chi Minh City to Phnom Penh by bus, a trip that takes approximately six hours, including a border stop. This crossing is called Moc Bai on the Vietnamese side and Bavet on the Cambodian side. Additionally, at least seven other land border crossings are open between the two nations; private vehicles are allowed to cross at all points. Visas on arrival are available at most land border crossings.

Water Crossings

The Mekong River runs north to south through Cambodia before ending in Vietnam. River boat border crossings are available with brief stops on land to check passports and visas. Visas on arrival are available at the Cambodian border crossing. Fast boat service is available from Chau Doc in Vietnam to Phnom Penh. More leisurely cruises are also available, and service is available all the way to Siem Reap in the north central part of Cambodia.

part3_passage.title: TOURS FROM MOC BAI INTERNATIONAL BORDER GATE
part3_passage.text: Moc Bai Border Gate in Tay Ninh province is the largest international land border in the south, along the mainland of Vietnam - Cambodia. Compared to the other border gates, Moc Bai has a special advantage because it is located on the trans-Asia road and only 70 km from Ho Chi Minh City. Along with tax-free supermarkets, Moc Bai has welcomed many tourists from Ho Chi Minh City and neighboring provinces to visit and shop. Near the border gate, Thuan Loi tunnel - the revolutionary historical site - is also an attractive destination. In addition, visitors can come to Ba Den Mountain, the highest mountain in Southeastern region, symbolizing the land and people of Tay Ninh to enjoy a panoramic view of Tay Ninh town center. About 7 km from Ha Tien town, Xa Xia or Ha Tien international border gate has long become a famous tourist attraction in Kien Giang. Going to Ha Tien, visitors also have the opportunity to enjoy many famous scenic spots such as Da Dung Mountain, Phu Dung Pagoda. Ha Tien is also the home to beautiful beaches such as Mui  Nai, Bai Bang, Bai No ... You can hire seats on the seaside for sunbathing, watching the waves, enjoying fresh air and seafood here.

part3_passage.title: TRA, VIETNAM TO FRANCE
part3_passage.text:
One day while shopping in a local market, Tra, a 17 year-old Vietnamese girl, met a man who claimed to represent a programme that sponsored Vietnamese girls to study in Europe. He was very friendly and gave her many details about the program. She thought it was an opportunity she could not pass up and, despite her father’s concerns, decide to go with the man to what she thought was a school in France. He promised her that it would be easy to learn French and that there would be other young international students in the house where she would live. Tra was eventually taken to France.

The man took away her passport and forced her to remain in the buildings where they stayed along the way. She was not allowed to contact her family on the entire trip. The journey took over a month. But when they finally reached their destination they were not at a school but at a brothel. The man sold Tra to the madam for U.S $ 10,000. She was forced to work as a prostitute for one and a half years under threat of violence if she did not meet her required daily earnings. She had to turn over all of her earnings to the madam.

One night, while working on the street, the police demanded to see Tra’s papers. The madam had not given her to the correct visa so she was detained and told she would be deported on the next flight out to Vietnam. The police made no effort to find out if Tra was a victim of trafficking, nor did they offer her any support or protection against potential harm from her traffickers.

part3_passage.title: PASSPORT EXAMINATION
part3_passage.text:
Millions of passports are examined every day to confirm the identity and nationality of the holders. A quick conversation often helps to resolve or identify problems quickly.

The first qualitative assessment done by the examiner concerns the cover of the passport. Traditionally the cover has the name of the issuing state and a well-defined image such as the national coat-of-arms printed in gold or silver. The text and images in a counterfeit document are usually far less precise.

The next step is to briefly examine the construction of the document. Begin with a check of pages, ensuring that they are all there, the same size and that all the edges are even. When for the binding stitching has been used, ensure that the thread is tight, and that it runs through all of the holes.

The data page varies by location in different passports and is the principal target of the forger. This page contains the photograph and the personal details of the bearer. The most important check to be done at this stage is to look at the picture and at the bearer. Is it the same person? Take note of physical features that do not change over time: the ears, the shape of the nostrils, the jaw and the setting of eyes.

part3_passage.title: FLIGHTS
part3_passage.text:
Most flights from Vietnam to Cambodia originate in Ho Chi Minh City and take between one to two hours. Arriving by air in Cambodia is relatively simple and the customs process is mostly hassle- free.

Land Crossings

Numerous land crossings are available between Vietnam and Cambodia. Many people travel from Ho Chi Minh City to Phnom Penh by bus, a trip that takes approximately six hours, including a border stop.

This crossing is called Moc Bai on the Vietnamese side and Bavet on the Cambodian side. Additionally, at least seven other land border crossings are open between the two nations; private vehicles are allowed to cross at all points. Visas on arrival are available at most land border crossings.

Water Crossings

Fast boat service is available from Chau Doc in Vietnam to Phnom Penh. More leisurely cruises are also available, and service is available all the way to Siem Reap in the north central part of Cambodia.

Moc Bai border gate

Moc Bai border gate in Tay Ninh province is the largest international land border in the south, along the mainland of Vietnam - Cambodia. Compared to the other border gates, Moc Bai has a special advantage because it is located on the trans-Asia road and only 70 km from Ho Chi Minh City. VISAS OVERSTAYING

According to Vietnam Immigration policy, foreigners wishing to enter Vietnam legally are required to have a valid Vietnam visa. However, in some cases, because of unexpected reasons, visa holders do not exit Vietnam or extend the visa even their Vietnam visas expire. In this case, foreigners will be penalized for their actions.

If traveler’s visa has been expired no more than 3 days, besides visa extension fee, the holder must pay penalty fee for overstaying about 500.000 VND per day overstaying (about 25 USD). For foreigners planning to exit Vietnam after overstaying for no more than 3 days, they can pay fine fee by themselves to Vietnam Immigration officer at ports of exit, and then leave the country.

part3_passage.title: VISA PROCEDURES
part3_passage.text:
Most visitors to Vietnam need a visa to enter the country. Visas are exempted for the citizens of the countries, which have signed a bilateral or unilateral visa exemption agreement with Vietnam, tourist visa may be valid for 15 to 30 days.

Tourist visa can only be obtained in Vietnamese embassies,  consulates abroad. Otherwise, you can get a visa at the border gates if you are invitees of some Vietnamese competent organs or you are travelling on a package tour organized by Vietnamese travel companies. To apply for a visa, the requirements are as follows:

Entry permit form. There are two categories of this form, one for foreigners and one for overseas Vietnamese, two photos (4cmx6cm or 3cmx4cm), original valid passport and visa fee.

You should send your application and photos to a Vietnamese embassy or consulate abroad, which is most convenient to you. On your requirement, the reply will be returned by post (you must provide stamped envelopes with your name and address). It is easier to get your visa from a Vietnamese travel agency. The necessary information to the travel agency include: full name, date and place of birth, nationality,  job, number of your passport, entry and exit dates, country you plan to receive your entry visa from the Vietnamese embassies or consulates.
